title: Widen skeleton: claim/release/finish with events and locking
description: Add the mutation half of the skeleton — the first commands that write. This forces the core write path: acquire a local write lock (proper-lockfile), validate input, write the JSON record atomically, append a JSONL event, and invalidate or update the bun:sqlite index. Implement `task claim`, `task release`, and `task finish`.
acceptance: `waystation task claim <id> --agent <a>` creates an active claim JSON record and appends a task.claimed event.
`waystation task release <id>` and `waystation task finish <id>` update claim/task state and append the matching events (claim.released / claim.completed / task.status_changed).
A second active claim on the same task is rejected (no force flag in V1).
Writes go through a single core write path shared with future CLI/dashboard/MCP callers (spec §12), are atomic, and hold the write lock.
bun:test covers a claim/finish round trip and the double-claim rejection; events.jsonl remains valid JSONL after mutations.
notes: This is where the append-only event model and write safety become real. Keep the write path in core so the later dashboard and MCP layers reuse it rather than re-implementing writes.
